prompt: Matcher.group throws IllegalStateException. What is the bug?
choices: group() requires the pattern to match the full string; use matches() instead of find()
group() throws IllegalStateException when called before a successful find() or matches(); a match attempt must succeed before group data is available
Matcher.group() returns null when there is no match in the string
Pattern.compile must specify flags to enable group capture
explanation: Matcher.group() accesses the result of the most recent match attempt. Calling it before find() or matches() (or when the last attempt failed) throws IllegalStateException. Fix: call m.find() first and check the return value: if (m.find()) System.out.println(m.group());
